creator: asami@cs.berkeley.edu
text:
* Since I not install all ports (linuxgdb and scogdb) I even can't detect
 * this problem. It looks like error in those ports to install libreadline
 * without renaming it. They must use system one or rename private version to
 * something else.  BTW, from where *.so variant appearse? 

I'm not asking you to detect the problem, I already did it for you. :)

Yes, I agree that they probably shouldn't install libreadline, but
this is such a commonly used library that have been included in so
many software over the years I think we should make sure our ports
don't trip over it.

I have no idea why the *.so was installed too (and they aren't even in 
the PLIST).  I'll have to investigate.

 * IMHO, proper solution will be to fix linuxgdb and scogdb ports instead to
 * not bload reserved namespace. No libreadline or another commonly used
 * library name (libc? :-) must be in /usr/local/lib for GNU configure. 

Let's do both, Andrey, both.  I'll get the *gdb ports fixed, you get
the ncftp2 fixed.  I'm not sure if that's the only one affected, at
least that's the only one reported so far.

It's not only the ports, users might install some software (like an
unported bash-3.0beta or whetever) that puts libreadline in
/usr/local/lib.  It can't hurt to be cautious, can it?

Satoshi
